# Source: NMP69524 SOW Annexure C- AIC Schedule.DOCX
# Document Type: docx
# Normalised For: Tender agent ingestion

Annexure C

australian industry capability SCHEDULE

Note to drafters:  This annex should be used for procurements where the expected value of any resultant Work Order will be between $4 million and $20 million (GST inclusive).

If the expected value of any resultant Work Order is likely to exceed $20 million, then the AIC Plan template should be used.

Note to tenderers:  The Australian Industry Capability (AIC) Schedule forms part of any resultant Work Order and describes the Supplier’s commitments to the participation of Australian Industry for that Work Order, including local businesses operating in the areas where work is undertaken, and participation by other small and

document: NMP69524 SOW Annexure E- AIC Plan Description.md

chunks.chunk_preview:
# Source: NMP69524 SOW Annexure E- AIC Plan Description.DOCX
# Document Type: docx
# Normalised For: Tender agent ingestion

NOTE TO TENDERERS AND SUPPLIERS: 	This Data Item Description is provided for guidance only. The draft AIC Plan must be submitted to the Commonwealth in accordance with any timescales specified in Item 30 of an RFQ or Work Order. The final AIC Plan Approved by the Commonwealth will replace this guidance document as the new Annex x to the Statement of Work. Guidance on completion of the AIC Plan and associated definitions for the AIC terminology used in this AIC Plan Description is provided on the AIC webpage available at: http://www.defence.gov.au/SPI/Industry/AIC.asp.

TITLE:	Australian Industry CAPABILITY PLAN

DESCRIPTION and intended use

The Australian Industry C

wealth’s internet website.

The initial Public AIC Plan is to be developed from the successful RFQ response and any negotiated changes prior to being submitted to the Commonwealth for Approval.

Updates to the Public AIC Plan are to be prepared, in accordance with the requirements of the Statement of Work and Work Order, to ensure that the plan accurately reflects forthcoming industry opportunities for publication on the Commonwealth internet website.

Generic Format and content

The Public AIC Plan shall be written on a company letterhead, signed by a duly authorised officer of the Supplier, and presented in a format that can be published on a Commonwealth internet website.

The Public AIC Plan shall only contain information of an appropriate security classification for publication on a p
